prompt: abstract elegant continuous line drawing of a bird in flight, flapping its wings, thick white line on black background, hand drawn feeling

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Abstract Continuous Line Bird</title>
    <style>
        body { margin: 0; overflow: hidden; background-color: #000; }
        canvas { display: block; width: 100vw; height: 100vh; }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <script type="module">
        import * as THREE from 'https://esm.run/three';
        import { OrbitControls } from 'https://esm.run/three/examples/jsm/controls/OrbitControls.js';

        // Scene Setup
        const scene = new THREE.Scene();
        scene.background = new THREE.Color(0x000000);
        scene.fog = new THREE.FogExp2(0x000000, 0.05);

        const camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(45, window.innerWidth / window.innerHeight, 0.1, 100);
        camera.position.set(0, 5, 10);

        const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer({ antialias: true });
        renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight);
        renderer.setPixelRatio(2); // High DPI
        document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement);

        const controls = new OrbitControls(camera, renderer.domElement);
        controls.enableDamping = true;
        controls.autoRotate = true;
        controls.autoRotateSpeed = 2.0;

        // Bird Configuration
        const segments = 100; // Smoothness of the curve
        const tubeRadius = 0.06; // Thickness of the line
        const radialSegments = 8; // Roundness of the tube
        
        // Material - Pure white, unlit for "drawing" effect
        const material = new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ 
            color: 0xffffff,
            side: THREE.DoubleSide
        });

        // Geometry container
        let geometry;
        let mesh;

        // Bird Shape Logic
        // We define key points of the bird: Head, Shoulders, Wing Joints, Wing Tips, Tail
        // The path creates a continuous loop.
        function getBirdPoints(time) {
            const t = time * 5; // Flapping speed
            
            // Flapping Math
            // Core body oscillation (slight bobbing)
            const bodyY = Math.sin(t * 0.5) * 0.2;
            
            // Wing motion: Inner wing moves less than tip. Phase shift for fluidity.
            const wingY = Math.sin(t); 
            const tipY = Math.sin(t - 0.5) * 2.5; 
            
            // Coordinates: X (Left/Right), Y (Up/Down), Z (Forward/Back)
            
            // 1. Head
            const p1 = new THREE.Vector3(0, 0.5 + bodyY, 2.5);
            
            // 2. Right Neck/Shoulder
            const p2 = new THREE.Vector3(0.5, 0.2 + bodyY, 1.5);
            
            // 3. Right Wing Elbow
            const p3 = new THREE.Vector3(2.5, wingY + bodyY, 0.5);
            
            // 4. Right Wing Tip (The furthest point)
            const p4 = new THREE.Vector3(5.0, tipY + bodyY, -1.0);
            
            // 5. Right Wing Back (Looping back towards tail)
            const p5 = new THREE.Vector3(2.0, wingY * 0.5 + bodyY, -1.5);
            
            // 6. Tail
            const p6 = new THREE.Vector3(0, -0.2 + bodyY + Math.sin(t-1)*0.3, -3.0);
            
            // 7. Left Wing Back
            const p7 = new THREE.Vector3(-2.0, wingY * 0.5 + bodyY, -1.5);
            
            // 8. Left Wing Tip
            const p8 = new THREE.Vector3(-5.0, tipY + bodyY, -1.0);
            
            // 9. Left Wing Elbow
            const p9 = new THREE.Vector3(-2.5, wingY + bodyY, 0.5);
            
            // 10. Left Neck/Shoulder
            const p10 = new THREE.Vector3(-0.5, 0.2 + bodyY, 1.5);

            return [p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7, p8, p9, p10];
        }

        // Initialize Mesh
        const initialCurve = new THREE.CatmullRomCurve3(getBirdPoints(0), true); // true = closed loop
        geometry = new THREE.TubeGeometry(initialCurve, segments, tubeRadius, radialSegments, true);
        mesh = new THREE.Mesh(geometry, material);
        scene.add(mesh);

        // Animation Loop
        const clock = new THREE.Clock();

        function animate() {
            requestAnimationFrame(animate);
            
            const time = clock.getElapsedTime();

            // Update Bird Geometry
            // Since TubeGeometry cannot easily update path in-place without complex logic,
            // and the mesh is low-poly (abstract), regenerating it is acceptable for this demo.
            
            const newPoints = getBirdPoints(time);
            const curve = new THREE.CatmullRomCurve3(newPoints, true, 'catmullrom', 0.5);
            
            // Update the existing geometry
            mesh.geometry.dispose();
            mesh.geometry = new THREE.TubeGeometry(curve, segments, tubeRadius, radialSegments, true);

            controls.update();
            renderer.render(scene, camera);
        }

        // Resize Handler
        window.addEventListener('resize', () => {
            camera.aspect = window.innerWidth / window.innerHeight;
            camera.updateProjectionMatrix();
            renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight);
        });

        animate();
    </script>
</body>
</html>

systemInstructions: You are an expert Three.js developer. When given a prompt, you will use your creativity and coding skills to create a Three.js scene that perfectly satisfies the prompt. Always return a full HTML document with the Three.js library included. Import the library and any other necessary libraries via the esm.run CDN (e.g. https://esm.run/three). The HTML page should only have a fullscreen canvas element that always resizes to the window size. Remember to set the renderer.setPixelRatio to 2. Always add orbit controls to the scene so the user can rotate the camera. Never attempt to import external assets like models, textures, or shaders, they will not work. Break down object requests into simple primitive shapes. Render them as wireframes. Auto-rotate the camera around the objects. Always use a pure black background. Return ONLY the full HTML page, nothing else, no commentary.
